sentences.base_language: Good morning. Please come in and have a seat.
sentences.target_language: صباح الخير. تفضل بالدخول واجلس من فضلك.
sentences.romanized: sabah al-khayr. tafaddal bil-dukul wa-ijlis min fadlak.
sentences.literal: Morning the-good. Be-welcome with-the-entering and-sit from your-favour.
sentences.grammarNote: تَفَضَّل (tafaḍḍal, please/go ahead) — Form V imperative of ف-ض-ل (f-ḍ-l, to be gracious): an invitation to proceed. بِالدُّخُول (bid-dukhūl, with entering) — بِـ + verbal noun Form I of د-خ-ل (to enter). اِجلِس (ijlis, sit! — Form I imperative of ج-ل-س, to sit): the imperative is formed by dropping the prefix يَـ and adding a connecting vowel اِـ before consonant clusters.

sentences.base_language: Good morning. Thank you for this opportunity.
sentences.target_language: صباح الخير. شكراً لك على هذه الفرصة.
sentences.romanized: sabah al-khayr. shukran laka 'ala hadhihi al-fursa.
sentences.literal: Morning the-good. Thank-you for-you on this the-opportunity.
sentences.grammarNote: شكراً لَكَ (shukran laka) = thank you (to a man). Arabic distinguishes grammatical gender: لَكَ (laka, to you m.) vs. لَكِ (laki, to you f.). This formal register is used throughout a job interview.

sentences.base_language: Can you tell me about your previous work experience?
sentences.target_language: هل يمكنك أن تحدثني عن خبرتك العملية السابقة؟
sentences.romanized: hal yumkinuka an tuhaddithani 'an khibratika al-'amaliyya as-sabiqa?
sentences.literal: Q possible-for-you that you-tell-me about your-experience the-practical the-previous?
sentences.grammarNote: تُحَدِّثَنِي (tuḥaddīthanī, you tell me) — Form II of ح-د-ث (ḥ-d-th, to occur/narrate): حَدَّثَ = to tell/narrate + suffix نِي (me). خُبرَتَك العَمَلِيَّة السَّابِقَة (khibrataka al-ʿamaliyya as-sābiqa, your practical previous experience) — a noun with two adjectives, both taking الـ to agree with the definite خُبرَة. العَمَلِيَّة (practical) is a nisba adjective from عَمَل; السَّابِقَة (previous) from root س-ب-ق (to precede).

sentences.base_language: I worked for three years as an accountant at a company downtown.
sentences.target_language: عملت لمدة ثلاث سنوات محاسباً في شركة في وسط المدينة.
sentences.romanized: amiltu li-muddat thalath sanawat muhasiban fi sharika fi wasat al-madina.
sentences.literal: I-worked for-duration three years accountant in company in middle the-city.
sentences.grammarNote: ثَلَاث سَنَوَات (thalāth sanawāt, three years) — ثَلَاث (three, feminine form) + سَنَوَات (years, sound feminine plural of سَنَة). For numbers 3–10, the number takes the opposite gender of the noun (gender polarity). مُحَاسِباً (muḥāsiban, as an accountant — accusative) — active participle Form III of ح-س-ب (to count/reckon): حَاسَبَ = to settle accounts. وَسَط المَدِينَة (wasaṭ al-madīna, downtown) — iḍāfa: centre + city.

ما هي مَهَارَاتُكَ؟ (mā hiya mahārātuka, what are your skills?) — مَا (what) + هِي (she/they, feminine copula) + definite subject: a formal question structure. مَهَارَات (mahārāt, skills) — sound feminine plural of مَهَارَة (skill, Form IV verbal noun of م-ه-ر, to be skilled): مَهَرَ → أَمهَرَ. الرَّئِيسِيَّة (ar-raʾīsiyya, main) — nisba adjective from رَئِيس (chief/president).

sentences.base_language: I am skilled in organising data and communicating with clients.
sentences.target_language: أنا بارع في تنظيم البيانات والتواصل مع العملاء.
sentences.romanized: ana bari' fi tanzim al-bayanat wat-tawasul ma'a al-umala'.
sentences.literal: I skilled in organising the-data and-the-communication with the-clients.
sentences.grammarNote: الواو (wa-) joins two nouns here: التنظيم (at-tanzim = organising) والتواصل (wat-tawasul = and-communication). When و is followed by ال, the two merge: و + ال → وال, pronounced wal-.

sentences.base_language: Why do you want to work at our company?
sentences.target_language: لماذا تريد أن تعمل في شركتنا؟
sentences.romanized: limadha turid an ta'mal fi sharikatina?
sentences.literal: Why you-want that you-work in our-company?
sentences.grammarNote: لِمَاذَا (limādhā, why) — the question word لِـ (for what) + مَاذَا (what): literally 'for what reason?' شَرِكَتِنَا (sharikatinā, our company) — شَرِكَة (company, from root ش-ر-ك, partnership) + suffix نَا (our). The ة becomes ت before suffixes. تُرِيد أَن تَعمَل (turīd an taʿmal, you want to work) — the standard Arabic 'want to do' pattern: verb + أَن + subjunctive.

sentences.base_language: Because your company has an excellent reputation and I want to develop my skills.
sentences.target_language: لأن شركتكم تتمتع بسمعة ممتازة وأريد تطوير مهاراتي.
sentences.romanized: li'anna sharikatkum tatamatta' bi-sum'a mumtaza wa-urid tatwir maharati.
sentences.literal: Because your-company enjoys with-reputation excellent and-I-want developing my-skills.
sentences.grammarNote: لِأَنَّ (li'anna) = because (followed by a noun or pronoun). Compare with لِأَنَّ + noun: لِأَنَّ شركتكم... = because your company... This is different from لأنَّني (li'annani = because I…), where the pronoun is attached as a suffix.

sentences.base_language: Do you have any questions for us?
sentences.target_language: هل لديك أي أسئلة تودّ طرحها علينا؟
sentences.romanized: hal ladayka ayy as'ila tawaddu tarhaha 'alayna?
sentences.literal: Q for-you any questions you-wish putting-them on-us?
sentences.grammarNote: أَسئِلَة (asʾila, questions) — broken plural of سُؤَال (suʾāl, question), pattern أَفعِلَة. From root س-أ-ل (s-ʾ-l, to ask): سَأَلَ (to ask), سُؤَال (question), مَسأَلَة (matter/issue). تَوَدُّ طَرحَها (tawaddu ṭarḥahā, you wish to raise them) — تَوَدُّ from root و-د-د (to wish): Form I, + طَرحَها (raising them): verbal noun + suffix هَا (them, referring to أَسئِلَة). عَلَينَا (ʿalaynā, on us) — عَلى + نَا.

sentences.base_language: Yes — what does a typical working day look like here?
sentences.target_language: نعم — كيف يبدو يوم العمل الاعتيادي هنا؟
sentences.romanized: na'am — kayfa yabdu yawm al-'amal al-i'tiyadi huna?
sentences.literal: Yes — how it-appears day the-work the-typical here?
sentences.grammarNote: كَيفَ يَبدُو (kayfa yabdū, how does it appear/look) — كَيف (how) + يَبدُو (Form I of ب-د-و, to appear): a defective verb whose و shows in the present tense. يَوم العَمَل (yawm al-ʿamal, working day) — iḍāfa: day + work. الاِعتِيَادِيّ (al-iʿtiyādī, the typical/usual) — nisba adjective from اِعتِيَاد (habit/custom), Form VIII verbal noun of ع-و-د (to return/be accustomed).

sentences.base_language: We will contact you within a week.
sentences.grammarNote: سـ (sa-) attached to a verb indicates the near future: سَنَتَواصَل (sa-natawassal) = we will contact. This prefix is more definite than سوف (sawfa), which can imply a more distant future.

sentences.base_language: Thank you very much. I hope to hear from you soon.
sentences.target_language: شكراً جزيلاً. آمل أن أسمع منك قريباً.
sentences.romanized: shukran jazilan. amal an asma' minka qariban.
sentences.literal: Thanks abundant. I-hope that I-hear from-you soon.
sentences.grammarNote: آمُل أَن أَسمَع (āmul an asmaʿ, I hope to hear) — آمُل from root أ-م-ل (ʾ-m-l, to hope): أَمَلَ (to hope), أَمَل (hope/aspiration). مِنكَ (minka, from you m.) — مِن (from) + suffix كَ (you). قَرِيباً (qarīban, soon) — indefinite accusative adverb from قَرِيب (near/soon), root ق-ر-ب (q-r-b, to be near): قَرُبَ (to draw near), قُربَى (kinship).
